intro: Soehrensia bruchii, commonly known as Bruch's Soehrensia, is a small, succulent plant native to the Andes mountain range in South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Soehrensia bruchii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
